macro_events.event_summary: Treasury Secretary Bessent revealed many US allies have requested dollar currency swap lines amid Iran war-driven FX volatility, signaling dollar liquidity stress abroad and reinforcing USD reserve-currency demand.
primary_drivers: Global dollar shortage / flight-to-USD funding demand during Middle East conflict
Elevated FX and rates volatility from war risk premium
Defense/geopolitical risk premium embedded in energy and defense names

beneficiaries.rationale: Currency swap demand and FX volatility drive record FX futures and rates futures volumes at CME; war-driven hedging is a direct revenue tailwind via transaction fees.
Runs Brent crude complex and key FX/rates venues; Iran war turbulence plus allied hedging demand boosts energy and FX trading volumes directly tied to transaction revenue.
Iran war escalation (implied by Bessent framing it as an active 'war') sustains munitions/missile-defense demand; Lockheed's PAC-3 and THAAD are directly consumed in Middle East theater.
Iran war sustains elevated Brent risk premium; ExxonMobil's upstream leverage to crude prices is among the highest in US large-cap energy.
FX trading desk is a top-tier beneficiary of cross-currency basis stress and volatility; Goldman historically monetizes swap-line-era FX dislocations in its FICC segment.

losers.rationale: US airlines with transatlantic/Middle East routes face both higher jet fuel (Brent premium) and route disruption risk from active Iran war conditions.
Heavier international exposure than domestic peers; Middle East airspace disruption plus fuel spike is a direct earnings headwind.

headlines: Bessent says 'many' U.S. allies have asked for currency swaps amid Iran war turbulence

macro_events.event_summary: Trump is reportedly preparing to reclassify cannabis (likely Schedule I → Schedule III), which would remove the 280E tax penalty and ease banking/research restrictions for US operators.
primary_drivers: Removal of IRS Section 280E tax burden dramatically improves cash flow for US plant-touching operators
Regulatory de-risking drives institutional capital back to the sector
Path toward expanded legal market and banking access (SAFE-style reforms) becomes more credible

beneficiaries.rationale: Named in the headline; Canadian LP with US beverage/hemp exposure — catches sector sentiment flow and is the most liquid retail cannabis vehicle.
Canopy Growth has structured US option on Acreage Holdings specifically to capitalize on US federal rescheduling; rescheduling triggers re-rating of that optionality.
Scotts Miracle-Gro's Hawthorne division sells hydroponics/cultivation supplies to growers; rescheduling accelerates legal cultivation expansion and ends the Hawthorne earnings drag.

losers.rationale: Constellation Brands (Corona, Modelo) — cannabis beverages become more competitive with alcohol as legal access broadens; also holds CGC equity stake but core beer franchise faces share loss risk over time.
Anheuser-Busch InBev faces the same alcohol-substitution risk as cannabis use normalizes under a less restrictive federal schedule.
GEO Group private prisons — lower federal drug scheduling reduces cannabis-related incarceration pipeline, a marginal negative for detention demand.

headlines: Tilray and other cannabis stocks soar as Trump is reportedly ready to reclassify the substance
